business_hours.seasonal_hours: Saturdays: 7 AM to 2 PM from April through September. Closed Saturdays from October through March.
business_hours.holiday_policy: Closed on all major holidays (New Year's, Memorial Day, July 4th, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas). Rick takes emergency calls on holidays for existing customers.

office_address: 3401 East McDowell Road, Suite 180, Phoenix, Arizona 85008

contact_info.name: Karen
contact_info.context: Manages the office for Summit HVAC Solutions and handles non-emergency after-hours follow-ups.

contact_info.name: Rick
contact_info.context: Owner of Summit HVAC Solutions, handles primary after-hours emergency calls.

contact_info.name: Danny
contact_info.context: Senior technician, serves as backup for after-hours emergency calls.

contact_info.name: Karen Wheeler
contact_info.context: Handles all dispatch for existing customers with service issues during business hours.

contact_info.name: Rick
contact_info.context: First contact for emergency routing after hours and on holidays for existing customers. Also handles specific requests during business hours.

contact_info.name: Danny
contact_info.context: Second contact in the emergency routing escalation chain after hours.

services_supported: Commercial and residential heating installation and repair
Thermostat and smart control system installation
Indoor air quality services (air purification, humidity control)
Rooftop unit service for commercial buildings
Refrigeration system repair for restaurants and commercial kitchens
services_excluded: Plumbing beyond what's needed for HVAC
Electrical beyond what's needed for HVAC
emergency_definition: Complete AC failure at any commercial property (restaurants, medical offices, schools, data centers)
Gas leaks or suspected gas leaks related to heating equipment
Carbon monoxide detector alerts related to HVAC systems
No-heat situations when outdoor temperature is below 40 degrees

emergency_routing_rules.fallback_action: Take a detailed message including name, business name, phone, address, exact system issue, and current temperature situation. Promise callback within 20 minutes.

non_emergency_routing_rules.action: Clara to answer calls, collect caller information, and inform them of callback.

call_transfer_rules.during_hours_transfer: Karen Wheeler (602-555-0134) for existing customers with service issues; Rick's cell (602-555-0156) for specific requests for Rick.

integration_constraints: Clara does not need to integrate with Housecall Pro for scheduling.
after_hours_flow_summary: During after-hours, Clara will answer calls. If the call is identified as an emergency based on predefined criteria (e.g., complete AC failure at commercial property, gas leaks, CO alerts, refrigerant leaks, no-heat below 40 degrees), Clara will first attempt to transfer to Rick (602-555-0156) for 60 seconds. If Rick does not answer, Clara will then attempt to transfer to Danny (602-555-0178). If Danny also does not answer, Clara will take a detailed message, collecting the caller's name, business name, phone, address, the exact system issue, and the current temperature situation, promising a callback within 20 minutes. For non-emergency calls after hours, Clara will take a standard message, collecting the caller's name, phone number, type of service needed, address, and whether they are an existing customer. Existing customers will be flagged for priority callbacks. Callers will be informed that they will receive a callback first thing on the next business day. On major holidays, Rick will take emergency calls specifically for existing customers.
office_hours_flow_summary: During business hours, Clara will answer all incoming calls and qualify the caller's intent. For existing customers calling with service issues, Clara will attempt to transfer the call directly to Karen Wheeler (602-555-0134), who handles all dispatch. For new customers inquiring about quotes, Clara will collect their contact information and details about their service needs, and Karen will call them back. If a caller specifically asks to speak with Rick, Clara will attempt to transfer the call to Rick's cell phone (602-555-0156). If Rick does not answer within 30 seconds, Clara will take a detailed message. Special rules apply: during the summer months (June, July, August), Clara must never promise same-day service for non-emergencies, instead stating that scheduling will occur 'as soon as possible.' Cool Club members should be identified and flagged as a priority. In the event a caller mentions a gas smell, Clara must immediately instruct them to leave the property, call 911 first, and then call Summit HVAC.
special_rules: Never promise same-day service for non-emergencies during summer (June, July, August); Clara should state scheduling will be 'as soon as possible'.
Cool Club members should be flagged as priority.
If a gas smell is mentioned, immediately instruct the caller to leave the property, call 911 first, then call Summit HVAC.
Rick takes emergency calls on holidays for existing customers.
Summit HVAC Solutions is licensed in Arizona and Nevada.
notes: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
